description: secretx-k8s/src/lib.rs:182, 209. The ZEROIZATION GAP comment at 164-167 acknowledges one unavoidable non-zeroized copy. The data.clone() at 182 (and 209 for SSA) clones the BTreeMap including its inner ByteString(Vec<u8>), creating a SECOND non-zeroized copy that lives until the patch_secret/apply_secret is dropped after serialization. Total live plaintext copies during a put: original Zeroizing<Vec<u8>> + bytes.to_vec() in BTreeMap + cloned BTreeMap inside K8sSecret = 3. Fix: only construct the K8sSecret once we know which path we are on. Move the K8sSecret construction into each match arm and use std::mem::take(&mut data) (or just data) when consuming for the actual call, then rebuild data on the create-fallthrough path. Rebuilding is cheap; widening the plaintext window is not.
title: data.clone() on patch path creates a third non-zeroized plaintext copy
